input: AI system infers workers' emotions during customer-service calls in a workplace.

expected.notes: v0.1.4 launch-feedback BLOCKER 3b (Codex reviewer retest of v0.1.3). v0.1.3 covered 'emotion recognition in the workplace' (5-token rigid form) but not natural paraphrases like 'infers workers' emotions during customer-service calls in a workplace'. The medical/safety carve-out invariant must continue to hold across all new paraphrases — the existing hasEmotionFCarveOut() rule-level check handles this; v0.1.4 only adds lexicon entries. New entries: 'infers workers emotions', 'infer workers emotions', 'detect workers emotions', 'analyze workers emotions', 'infer emotions in a workplace' + 3rd-person variants. annex_iii_domains is intentionally [] (not [1] like fixture-05): the v0.1.4 paraphrase doesn't independently match the annex_iii.1_biometrics lexicon entry 'emotion recognition' — Article 5(1)(f) prohibition is the sole trigger here, and suppression downgrades the Annex III high-risk path to nothing. This is honest pre-suppression behavior; if the input had named a biometric-recognition technology directly (e.g. 'emotion recognition for workers'), domain 1 would fire and the shape would mirror fixture-05. Cite: EUR-Lex Regulation (EU) 2024/1689, Article 5(1)(f).
